summary: Implement deterministic M5 simulation slice loop
acceptance_criteria: The accepted slice contract runs from start to success/failure through authoritative sim-core state and versioned GameCommand paths.
All changes preserve single-writer WorldState authority, deterministic stable IDs, explicit protocol/save schemas, player/AI command parity, and bounded serializable errors.

The loop reuses accepted M4 campaign, logistics, automatic engagement, siege, withdrawal, postwar handoff, and AI surfaces without adding manual node battle, full diplomacy, complex naval warfare, telemetry, server/multiplayer, or arbitrary-code scripting.
The same seed and command sequence produce the same state hash in Node runner and Worker-host contract coverage.
required_tests: Property/invariant tests cover player/AI command parity, stable ordering, save round-trip, rejection reasons, and no hidden authoritative mutation.
notes: Do not import React, Pixi, Electron, DOM, Node filesystem, or real-time APIs into sim-core.
Do not add a general manager, implicit RPC layer, object-graph serializer, arbitrary scripting, telemetry, server, or multiplayer substrate.
Architecture or save-format decisions beyond explicit schema evolution require ADR routing before implementation.
